title: CLAUDE.md
text: mizerEcopath bridges Ecopath (biomass-pool ecosystem models) mizer (size-spectrum models). goal initialise calibrate mizer models using Ecopath parameters (B, Q, P, C, DC) matching size-integrated mizer quantities Ecopath counterparts.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: newAllometricParams() — create allometric (power-law) MizerParams object starting point addEcopathParams() — attach Ecopath-derived parameters species_params matchEcopath() — master calibration: calls matchConsumption() matchProduction() iteratively matchCatch() — adjust gear selectivity catchability match observed catch size distributions (uses TMB optimisation, see src/objective_function.cpp) matchDiet() — set predation preferences match diet matrix alignResource() — scale resource spectrum match fish abundance tuneEcopath() — interactive Shiny gadget manual fine-tuning

title: CLAUDE.md
text: functions take MizerParams object return modified MizerParams (functional style). “Ecopath quantities” (B, Q/B, P/B, catch) computed integrating mizer size spectrum — see getConsumption(), getSomaticProduction(), getGonadicProduction(), getProduction(), getM0B(), getM2B(), getZB(), getDietMatrix(). matchExtMortOnce(), matchGonadicProportionOnce(), matchRespirationOnce() single-step helpers called iteratively higher-level matching functions. bindParams() combines multiple single-species MizerParams multi-species model.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: separate mathematical framework (PDE-based) models cohort growth diffusion fitting age--size data: - project_diffusion() → solve_diffusion_pde() → solve_double_sweep() (numerical PDE solver) - simulateCohort() single-cohort dynamics - plotAgeLikelihood(), getLogLik() age-data likelihood fitting - Supporting biology: age_mat_vB(), von_mises_pdf(), spawning_density(), length_rebinning_matrix()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: interactive gadget built composable pieces R/: - *Tab.R files define UI panels (growthTab, dietTab, deathTab, spectraTab, surveyTab, ageTab, catchTab, reproTab) - *Control.R files define control widgets (growthControl, matchControl, exponentControl, fishingControl, diffusionControl, otherControl, reproductionControl)

title: Celtic_Sea_Status_Quo_Model
text: Creating model representing average state Celtic Sea 2012 2024. Composed 15 species: herring (Clupea harengus), cod (Gadus morhua), haddock (Melanogrammus aeglefinus), whiting (Merlangius merlangus), blue whiting (Micromesistius poutassou), hake (Merluccius merluccius), monkfish (Lophius piscatorius), horse mackerel (Trachurus trachurus), mackerel (Scomber scombrus), plaice (Pleuronectes platessa), megrim (Lepidorhombus whiffiagonis), sole (Solea solea), boarfish (Capros aper), dragonet (Callionymus spp.), red gurnard (Chelidonicthys cuculus). Data includes: life_history parameters (l_mat age_mat calculated DATRAS data; w_max LWR Fishbase); landing distributions form STECF; survey size distribution age length distribution data DATRAS; Stomach contents data; Biomass estimates, yield estimates, interaction matrix, diet matrix Lauria (2016). Assign species parameters life history data

Boarfish <- ecopath_estimates[49, ] num_cols <- sapply(Gurnards, is.numeric) Red_gurnard<-Gurnards Other_Gurnards<-Gurnards Boarfish<-Boarfish Small_pelag<-Boarfish Red_gurnard[num_cols] <- recorded value observed_max <- catch_distribution %>%      group_by(species) %>%      summarise(          obs_len = if (all(is.na(length + dl))) NA_real_ else max(length + dl, na.rm = TRUE)      ) gurnard`      = "Red gurnard" )  sp <- sp_params sp <- addEcopathParams(sp, ecopath_params, species_to_groups)  adult_map <- tibble::tibble(   species      = names(species_to_groups),   adult_stanza = model model_non_int <- model_non_int %>%     steadySingleSpecies() %>%     matchBiomasses() %>%     steadySingleSpecies() %>%     matchBiomasses()%>%     steadySingleSpecies() %>%     matchBiomasses()%>%     steadySingleSpecies() %>%     matchBiomasses()%>%     steadySingleSpecies() %>%     matchBiomasses()  params<-model_non_int sps<-species_params(model_non_int) initial_Eiw<-array(dim=length(sps$species)) for(i in 1:length(sps$species)){     initial_Eiw[i]<-getExtEncounter(params)[i,1]/(w(params)[1]^sp[i,]$n)} sps$Eiw<-initial_Eiw species_params(model_non_int)<-sps  #set the spawning time, spawning concentration and mu mortality of species to predetermined values.  #spawning_mort<-tuneEcopath( #    model_non_int,  #    catch = all_fishing, #    age_at_length = cs_age_size)  #saveRDS(spawning_mort,"/Users/jessicawestworth/Desktop/spawning_mort.rds")

code: params <- validParams(celtic_params) species <- "Herring" w <- params@w  # Without diffusion params_no_diffusion <- params ps_no_diffusion <- mizer::steadySingleSpecies(params_no_diffusion, species) #> Warning in steadySingleSpecies.MizerParams(params_no_diffusion, species): #> Herring has zero growth rate after maturity size n_steady_no_diffusion <- ps_no_diffusion@initial_n[species, ]  # Set nonzero diffusion rate species_params(params)$d_over_g <- 0.15  # Set diffusion from d_over_g # d(w) = d_over_g * g(w) * w for (s in species_params(params)$species) {     d_over_g <- species_params(params)[s, "d_over_g"]     w <- params@w     growth <- getEGrowth(params)[s, ]     n <- params@species_params[s, "n"]     g_0 <- growth[1] / w[1]^n     d_0 <- d_over_g * g_0     diffusion(params)[s, ] <- d_0 * w^(n + 1) }  # Solve for steady state using the upwind scheme ps <- mizer::steadySingleSpecies(params, species) #> Warning in steadySingleSpecies.MizerParams(params, species): Herring has zero #> growth rate after maturity size n_steady <- ps@initial_n[species, ]  # Plot the resulting abundance density versus size plot(w, n_steady, log = "xy", type = "l", lwd = 2,      xlab = "Weight (g)", ylab = "Abundance density",      main = paste("Steady state solution for", species)) #> Warning in xy.coords(x, y, xlabel, ylabel, log): 64 y values <= 0 omitted from #> logarithmic plot lines(w, n_steady_no_diffusion, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2) p0 <- params p0@initial_n[species, ] <- n_steady_no_diffusion y0 <- plotYieldVsSize(p0, species = species, x_var = "Length",                       return_data = TRUE)[[1]] |>     dplyr::filter(Type == "Model catch") y0$Type <- "No diffusion"  p <- params p@initial_n[species, ] <- n_steady y <- plotYieldVsSize(p, species = species, x_var = "Length",                      return_data = TRUE)[[1]] |>     dplyr::filter(Type == "Model catch") y$Type <- "With diffusion" y <- rbind(y0, y)  ggplot(y, aes(x = l, y = `Catch density`, colour = Type)) +     geom_line()

title: Building a Mizer version of an Ecopath model
text: typical workflow creating mizer model Ecopath data involves following steps: Export Ecopath Parameters: Open Ecopath model “Ecopath Ecosim” export parameters CSV files. Prepare Species Table: Create table R one row species want include mizer model. table must contain: species: name species. w_max: largest observed size species. w_mat: weight maturity. ecopath_groups: corresponding Ecopath group(s). Initialize Model: Use newAllometricParams() create mizer model based parameters. sets model allometric encounter mortality rates, initially interactions. r     params <- newAllometricParams(species_params) Match Observations: Use various functions provided package matchBiomass() match total biomass species, matchGrowth() match growth rates, matchCatch() match total yield size distribution catch, matchConsumption() match total consumption species. Tune Model: Use interactive Shiny gadget tuneEcopath() fine-tune model reproduce observed size structure Ecopath parameters. r     params <- tuneEcopath(params) gadget allows interactively adjust parameters see effects growth, yield, consumption real-time. Set predation preferences: Use matchDiet() set predation preferences model match observed diet compositions. r     params <- matchDiet(params)

title: Building a Mizer version of an Ecopath model

title: Get diffusion rate — getDiffusion
text: diffusion rate \(d_i(w)\) species \(\) weight \(w\) contributions encounter fish prey resource. determined summing prey species resource spectrum integrating prey sizes \(w_p\), weighted predation kernel \(\phi(w,w_p)\): $$ d_i(w) = (1-f_i(w))(\alpha_i(1-\psi_i(w)))^2\gamma_i(w) \int \left( \theta_{ip} N_R(w_p) + \sum_{j} \theta_{ij} N_j(w_p) \right) \phi_i(w,w_p) w_p^2 \, dw_p. $$ \(N_j(w)\) abundance density species \(j\) \(N_R(w)\) abundance density resource. overall prefactor \(\gamma_i(w)\) determines predation power predator. interpreted search volume set mizer::setSearchVolume() function. predation kernel \(\phi(w,w_p)\) set mizer::setPredKernel() function. species interaction matrix \(\theta_{ij}\) set mizer::setInteraction() resource interaction vector \(\theta_{ip}\) taken interaction_resource column params@species_params. \(f(w)\) feeding level calculated mizer::getFeedingLevel(). \(\psi(w)\) proportion available energy invested reproduction instead growth, stored params@psi.

title: Match the model's production rate to empirical observations/estimates — matchProduction
text: Ecopath explicitly account loss due gonad production. However energy invested gonad production lost due inefficiency reproductive process. Therefore mizer need include loss respiration rate. Thus $$R = K + G - R_dd * w_0$$ \(K\) metabolic rate, \(G\) gonadic production rate, \(R_dd\) rate offspring production, \(w_0\) weight offspring size enters model. Thus \(G - R_dd * w_0\) net loss due gonad production. matchProduction function calls matchProductionOnce() repeatedly production matches within tolerance specified tol parameter. function return warning maximum number iterations reached without converging.

Solving \(R\) get: $$R = Q \alpha - P$$
